title: ulmus procera var. australis
intro: Ulmus procera var. australis, also known as the Australian Wych Elm, is a deciduous tree that belongs to the Ulmaceae family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it useful in treating various health conditions such as wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Ulmus procera var. australis include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and promote wound healing, while also providing antioxidant and antiseptic properties. The herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to create topical creams, ointments, and salves for external use, as well as teas and capsules for internal use.
